title: Request Authority, Host, Target Reconstruction, and Routing · Execution Trace: Tls Sni Host Boundary
summary: A server trusts any Host, accepts duplicate authority sources, reconstructs links from forwarded input, routes before validating the target, and confuses TLS identity with request authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tls sni host boundary increment in authority validation and target reconstruction table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish connection IP, TLS SNI, certificate identity, ALPN, HTTP Host or authority, and application tenant evidence.
Normalize host and port only as specified, validate against configured authority, then route, cache, authorize tenants, or construct links.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Tls Sni Host Boundary · Theory Tls Sni Host Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4372 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive and allowlist one effective request authority without trusting disagreement; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish connection IP, TLS SNI, certificate identity, ALPN, HTTP Host or authority, and application tenant evidence.
